page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz, the system features a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute and memory tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests fall within the middle 50% tier, with scores of 1437.59 and 11603.93 respectively. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 831.82 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 62.89 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% with a throughput of 35.65 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations under Passmark are weak at 2589.14. For lightweight machine learning, the server handles a small 135M LLM model with average speeds, but performance drops to poor when processing a medium 7B model, yielding only 5.01 tokens/sec during text generation.

The shared CPU allocation model offers a cost-effective hardware profile for multi-core workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU pinning. This resource density provides a budget-friendly option for horizontal scaling, though users must accept the tradeoff of potential noisy-neighbor performance fluctuations. Given the average performance in static web serving at 8.38 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 12.74 million operations/sec, this server is suited for general-purpose web serving, caching layers, and basic application hosting rather than heavy database operations or complex LLM inference.
description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring 8 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on a shared CPU allocation, it delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including stress-ng CPU tests and memory bandwidth. However, it exhibits performance tradeoffs, with weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations, alongside poor performance on medium 7B LLM inference. Qualitatively, the shared CPU model provides a cost-efficient entry point for multi-core workloads that tolerate resource sharing. It is best suited for general-purpose web serving, caching layers, and lightweight application hosting rather than resource-intensive database or machine learning tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is an x86_64 server with 8 shared Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced average performance for web serving and lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel features 8 shared Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe storage, delivering average compute performance.
tagline: An 8-core shared Intel Xeon virtual server with NVMe storage, delivering balanced performance for standard web serving and application hosting.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB system memory and 350 GB bundled NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in static web serving and Redis SET operations
Weak performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
Cost-efficient shared CPU allocation for non-continuous workloads
